forecasts.reasoning: Trump and Xi had a 'very good' phone call in early June 2025 during which they reciprocated invitations to visit each other's countries. Although Trump said he will visit China or teams will meet shortly, the calendar is very tight, with the deadline being July 1, 2025 (less than 3-4 weeks from the knowledge cutoff and the date of the call). Trump is scheduled to attend the G7 in Canada from June 15-17. In addition, previous early stage discussions focused on a potential summit in the US in June. Scheduling a state visit involving the leaders of the U.S. and China generally takes significant preparation, and while it's possible it could be rushed for late June (e.g. June 25-30), it seems relatively unlikely to happen before July 1 given the timeline. I assign a 25% probability that the visit actually occurs before the deadline.
